content_of_post_to_comment: One of the cruelest things people do is weaponize what you trusted them with. Not because they don’t know. But because they do. They know what hurt you. They know what broke your heart. They know what you survived. And somehow, when they want to hurt you, that’s exactly where they aim. That’s when you realize something painful. Not everyone who understands your pain respects it. Some people simply memorize it. And when the mask slips, you discover they weren’t holding your wounds with care. They were just saving them for later.
comment: The painful part is that vulnerability requires a certain kind of trust, but experience can teach us to confuse being understood with being safe. Those two things are not always the same.
